dialogue.utterance: Unit 3 responding to 1428 West Broad St. for an assault call.
Hi, I'm Ryan with EMS, and this is my partner Marc. What happened?
I found him in the bushes, his face was bleeding, and he said his head hurts.
Hey, sir, can you tell me where you are?
Eyes open to verbal stimuli. Sir, could you squeeze my hand?
Okay, you're moving, but it seems like you might be confused.
Can you tell me your name again, sir?
GCS is E2, V4, M6 - he's alert to verbal with confusion.
Airway is patent and clear. Marc, an IV line is going to need to be placed as we secure him.
Breathing appears normal, but we'll confirm. Marc, check for breath sounds and effort.
Bilateral breath sounds are clear and equal. No wheezes or stridor.
SpO2 is 97. Let me check the rest.
Respiration is 20, regular and effort looking normal.
Pulse is 123, strong and equal bilaterally.
Blood pressure is 120/72, normal so far.
Skin is warm and diaphoretic, sunburnt. Good radial and pedal pulses. No JVD noted.
Sir, we need to ask some questions to understand your history better. Can you tell us what happened?
Five guys jumped me with metal pipes in an alley. I ran and crashed my bike two blocks away.
Do you take any prescription medications or have any allergies?
No, I don't take any medications or have allergies.
Any medical conditions, like seizures or chronic ones?
I had a sandwich last night.
You said the back of your head hurts - when did it start?
Does anything make the pain better or worse?
What kind of pain is it? Throbbing, sharp, or pressure?
Does the pain travel anywhere else on your body?
If you can, rate the pain out of 10.
Has it gotten worse or stayed the same since it started?
I'll examine the cut under your chin and your swollen lip now.
I note no cervical spine tenderness or deformity, no step-offs. Let me check further.
Any drainage from your ears or trouble with your vision or hearing?
No hemotympanum or maxillofacial deformities found.
Let me listen to your lungs. Take a deep breath for me.
I'm applying a cervical collar now. Please stay still.
Examined all extremities. Equal strength bilaterally, no deformity or angulation.
There's a 2cm laceration below the chin and minor abrasions noted on both forearms.
No deformity, crepitus, abrasions, punctures, burns, or lacerations noted on T/L spine.
Protocol 4-1 (general trauma management) will be followed based on findings.
Glucose is 0, but recovered to 98 later at 14:54.
EKG recorded as sinus tachycardia. I'll keep monitoring.
We are placing you on 4-lead ECG monitoring for rhythm stability.
We are also applying etCO2 monitoring to support clinical decision making.
At 14:53, BP is 117/75, pulse at 111, respiration at 25, SpO2 is 95.
You still rate your head pain as 5?
At 14:54, BP is 116/72, pulse is 108, SpO2 is 95, respirations at 26.
You will be taken to [COMPANY_NAME] for evaluation and care.
You'll be in semi-Fowler's position with shoulder and lap belts on during transport.
IV started in the left AC with 20g catheter. Normal saline being administered.
At 14:54, glucose measurement is 98, recovered from initially 0 levels in early vitals.
At 15:08, vitals are BP 122/71, HR 110, SpO2 94, respirations at 26.
Is the pain still a 5/10?
IV functioning properly, patient remains stable in current state.
[COMPANY_NAME] medics, patient secured. Patient in transit, trauma case.
A cervical collar was applied per protocol to restrict motion.
ETA to [COMPANY_NAME] is 7 minutes.
[COMPANY_NAME] trauma team is standing by for arrival.
You have a history of epilepsy or seizures before?
Yes, I've had epilepsy and chronic pancreatitis.
[COMPANY_NAME] medics, trauma alarm is on. Patient is being sent for full trauma evaluation following assault.
Do you have a history of alcohol abuse or dependence?
Yes, alcohol dependence and intoxication.
The patient is uncooperative and intoxicated; history gathering is limited.
ETOH detected on his breath. Alcohol intoxication contributing to confusion and uncooperativeness.
Any seizures occurred either during or before the assault?
Just once in my history - not recently during the assault.
[COMPANY_NAME] transport vehicle is ready. All equipment is in place and patient is stable.
IV remains secure. Patient is comfortable and alert, as safely possible.
[COMPANY_NAME] care team, please be informed that this patient is being transported under S-Trauma protocol.
BP is still 122/71, HR is 110, and no added signs of distress.
[COMPANY_NAME] team, pre-arrival report dispatched. Trauma bay is on standby.
[COMPANY_NAME] team, patient safe and secured. Directed to trauma bay upon arrival.
Trauma protocols maintained and all measures are complete.
Patient's care will be transferred to [COMPANY_NAME] trauma team upon arrival.
No change observed since last assessment. Procedures remain per protocol.
Patient's assault history and intoxication will be relayed on handoff to [COMPANY_NAME] team.
IV and EKG maintaining stability since in-transit. Patient en route with full monitoring.
Bilateral motor, sensory, and reflexes are present across all four limbs.
No deformity, crepitus, abrasions, punctures, burns, or lacerations noted on T/L spine.
No tenderness, deformity, or step-offs identified along T/L spine.
Skin turgor appears normal; patient stays alert to verbal and pain stimuli.
We'll position you slightly now. Any issues with turning or other pain on movement?
Bilateral breath sounds remain clear and equal without wheeze or adventitious worsening.
No signs of deformity or injury found on torso - abdominal tenderness present but no discoloration or distention.
Flank and pelvic exams stable; no deformity or instability noted.
Baseline motor strength remains normal in all extremities and equal bilaterally.
Visual inspection of wounds shows no active bleeding other than laceration at the chin and minor abrasions on forearms.
Patient's sunburned skin is dry and taut with no blisters or ulcers in areas inspected.
At repeat assessment, vitals continue to trend in stable range.
Your head pain remains at 5? No increase or additional new areas reported?
Vitals continue stable during transport phase; 4-lead rhythm non-changing since 14:53.
Patient maintained - primary team on route, secondary team safe in transit.
Transport cage secured with appropriate moves for spine protection.
En route with full field monitor, 4-lead EKG, IV, and pre-arrival report on [COMPANY_NAME] line.
No new findings in secondary assessment or verbal responses through transport window.
O2 remains at 90% or over, HR in sinus tachycardia with no reported chest pain.
[COMPANY_NAME] trauma team has been advised of patient status and planned arrival time.
Trauma protocol maintained and all interventions per-assessment.
IV remains patent and secure with no signs of infiltration.
No new signs or symptoms from neuro to extremity noted during 4-lead monitoring.
Destined to trauma bay at [COMPANY_NAME] with all supporting data logs and documentation.
